headlines.title: Brent Breaks $96 as U.S. Strikes Iran for 12th Consecutive Night
headlines.summary: The latest oil price rally showed no sign of stopping in early Asian trading on Thursday, with both benchmarks climbing to their highest levels in more than six weeks as the U.S. and Iran continued to exchange strikes. At the time of writing, WTI crude was trading at $88.25 per b

headlines.title: Global Refiners Are Cutting Out Oil Traders To Buy Venezuelan Crude Directly
headlines.summary: Commodity traders are having the rug pulled out from under one of their biggest paydays yet as refiners start bypassing oil and traders and buying Venezuelan crude directly, according to Reuters. Refiners and major oil-producing firms are rapidly gaining market share in Venezuela

headlines.title: New Pipelines Set To Ease Permian Natural Gas Glut
headlines.summary: The regional price of natural gas produced in the Permian, the top U.S. oil basin, was negative for most of the first half of the year.
